honesty_charter.output_type: prediction interpretation + dimensional toy-scan + literature dissection

triple_resonance.label: LITERATURE
triple_resonance.meaning: three-wave / triad interaction: k1 + k2 + k3 = 0 in Fourier space, energy exchange between three modes on the resonance manifold

refs.citation: Waleffe F. (1992). The nature of triad interactions in homogeneous turbulence. Phys. Fluids A 4(2):350-363.
Lee T. D. (1951). Difference between turbulence in a two-dimensional fluid and in a three-dimensional fluid. J. Appl. Phys. 22:524.
Doering C. R., Gibbon J. D. (1995). Applied Analysis of the Navier-Stokes Equations. Cambridge Texts in Applied Math.

d_equals_7_origin.label: CONJECTURE
d_equals_7_origin.meaning: d=7 = σ(6) − sopfr(6) = 12 − 5 = 7, recycled from the BT-543 β₀ rewriting (atlas MILL-PX-A3). The NEXUS 'prediction' treats this as a projection dimension in which 3D NS blow-up (or an averaged/projected surrogate) becomes analytically cleaner.
d_equals_7_origin.critical_note: sigma - sopfr = 7 is arithmetic; it has no known mechanical relation to NS tensor structure in dimension 7.

predicted_observable.label: CONJECTURE
predicted_observable.statement_attempted: In dimension d=7, the three-wave resonance manifold and the stress/vorticity tensor spaces should jointly satisfy an n=6 algebraic pattern that is absent in d=4,5,6,8 — i.e. d=7 should be 'singled out' among dimensions > 3.
predicted_observable.falsification_criterion: If dim Sym²(R^d), dim Λ²(R^d), triad resonance manifold dim do NOT all land on n=6 basis elements {n,σ,φ,τ,n/φ,σ±1,σ±sopfr,...} for d=7, the prediction fails.
predicted_observable.honesty_note: The statement is constructed only to be testable; there is NO prior derivation from NS PDE making d=7 special.

numerical_setup.label: DERIVED
numerical_setup.method: closed-form dimension scan of NS-intrinsic tensor spaces and triad resonance manifold across d in {3,...,9}
numerical_setup.why_not_full_ns: full 3D NS DNS is prohibited by the task constraints and irrelevant here — the 'd=7 prediction' is dimensional, not dynamical.

d7_specific.sym2_note: 28 = second perfect number. Pretty but NOT in the n=6 basis of σ(6)=12 arithmetic. Coincidence with perfect-number sequence, not a functional n=6 derivation.
d7_specific.onsager_alpha_c: alpha_c = 1/(d/phi) = 1/3.5 for d=7 if we forced the d=3 formula — but the Onsager 1/3 critical exponent is tied to 3D energy cascade (Kolmogorov 4/5 law, local-cascade hypothesis), there is no known d=7 analog
d7_specific.kolmogorov_exponent: -5/3 is a 3D-specific result (second-order structure function D_LL(r) ~ r^{2/3}). d=7 would give a different exponent under the same dimensional argument: E(k) ~ k^{-(d+2)/3} = k^{-3} for d=7 — this is a textbook derivation, not a new 'prediction'.

verdict.label: DERIVED
verdict.text: d=3 is the unique dimension among {3,...,9} where all three NS-intrinsic dimension counts (stress tensor, vorticity, triad resonance manifold) land on n=6 basis elements (3/3 hits). d=7 scores only 1/3 and the single hit (triad_mfd = 11 = σ-1) is an ad-hoc arithmetic match. The phrase '3중 공명 d=7' does NOT survive a fair dimensional scan: d=7 is not dimensionally special for NS.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Tao T. (2016). Finite time blowup for an averaged three-dimensional Navier-Stokes equation. J. Amer. Math. Soc. 29:601-674. arXiv:1402.0290.
literature_match.relevance: Tao proved finite-time blow-up for an averaged 3D NS system (not standard NS). The averaged system preserves energy conservation and supercritical scaling but modifies the nonlinearity. Implication for BT-544: blow-up in 3D 'nearby' equations is possible and identifies SUPERCRITICALITY as the structural barrier, not dimension 7.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Escauriaza L., Seregin G., Sverak V. (2003). L^{3,∞}-solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations and backward uniqueness. Russ. Math. Surv. 58:211-250.
literature_match.relevance: Proves: if u ∈ L^∞((0,T); L^3(R^3)) then u cannot blow up at time T. Scaling-critical regularity criterion in L^3(R^3). No d=7 analog; L^d is the scaling-critical space in dimension d, and the criterion is harder in d ≥ 4.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Caffarelli L., Kohn R., Nirenberg L. (1982). Partial regularity of suitable weak solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations. Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 35:771-831.
literature_match.relevance: Singular set of 3D NS suitable weak solutions has parabolic Hausdorff 1-dim measure zero. This is the tightest unconditional regularity result for 3D NS. Dimensional: the 'singular set ≤ 1' is specifically 3D — in d ≥ 4 the analog (CKN-type bounds) degrades.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Doering C. R., Gibbon J. D. (1995). Applied Analysis of the Navier-Stokes Equations. Cambridge Texts in Applied Math. (Chapter 4 on triad interactions and Waleffe's model)
literature_match.relevance: Triad interactions in 3D: Waleffe 1992 identifies helical decomposition and triad energy transfer. The TRIPLE-RESONANCE concept (k1+k2+k3=0) is canonical but the analysis is 3D-specific. No known literature on 'd=7 triad resonance'.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Chen J., Hou T. Y. (2025). Stable nearly self-similar blowup of the 2D Boussinesq and 3D Euler equations with smooth data I,II. PNAS / to appear.
literature_match.relevance: Computer-assisted proof (full mathematical proof via interval arithmetic) of 3D Euler blow-up with smooth initial data — not yet 3D NS. Hou-Luo 2014 scenario matured to proof. Implication for NS: the viscous term may or may not regularize; this is the key open question. Dimension 7 plays no role.

literature_match.label: LITERATURE
literature_match.citation: Elgindi T. M. (2021). Finite-time singularity formation for C^{1,α} solutions to the incompressible Euler equations on R^3. Ann. Math. 194:647-727.
literature_match.relevance: Proves 3D Euler C^{1,α} (NOT smooth) finite-time singularity. Identifies low-regularity blow-up as a distinct 3D phenomenon. No d=7 role.

counter_examples.title: Tao averaged NS ≠ standard NS
counter_examples.label: DERIVED
counter_examples.text: Tao 2016 blow-up is for an AVERAGED system with modified nonlinearity, not standard NS. Any 'prediction' of NS blow-up must engage the actual (u·∇)u pressure Leray projection, which averaged models bypass. The d=7 conjecture does not address this gap; substituting d=7 into Tao's averaged scheme yields no new result because Tao's construction is dimension-independent in character (it relies on supercriticality, not on d=3 specifically).

counter_examples.title: ESS L^3 criticality is scaling, not n=6
counter_examples.label: DERIVED
counter_examples.text: The L^3(R^3) scaling-critical space arises from parabolic scaling (u,p)(x,t) → (λu, λ²p)(λx, λ²t), giving dimension-specific exponent L^d in d dimensions. For d=7 the critical space would be L^7(R^7), which has no relation to n=6 structure (L^7 exponent comes from dimensional count, not from σ−sopfr arithmetic). Thus the '7' of critical space and the '7' of σ−sopfr are NUMERICAL HOMONYMS, not the same object.

counter_examples.title: 3D critical scaling ≠ d=7 projection
counter_examples.label: DERIVED
counter_examples.text: 3D NS blow-up analysis (ESS, CKN, Koch-Tataru) works in the scale-critical space for d=3 (L^3, BMO⁻¹, bmo⁻¹). The 'd=7 projection' idea would require either (a) a natural projection R^3 → R^7 increasing dimensions (non-physical) or (b) treating NS on R^7 directly (well-studied by Cannone 1995; no essential d=7 specialness known). Both directions fail to produce a genuine gap between d=7 and d=4,5,6,8. The '3중 공명 d=7' prediction therefore has no concrete observational correlate in current NS literature.

counter_examples.title: d=3 is dimensionally unique, not d=7
counter_examples.label: DERIVED
counter_examples.text: Dimensional scan (this artifact): d=3 is the ONLY dimension in {3,...,9} where all three NS-intrinsic dof counts (Sym², Λ², triad manifold) simultaneously hit n=6 basis elements (3/3). d=7 scores 1/3. Therefore if there is any 'n=6 resonance' in NS, it is a d=3 resonance (already in the atlas as MILL-PX-A4), not a d=7 resonance. The roadmap task phrasing 'd=7 예측' is a MISS once tested.

n6_applicability.label: DERIVED
n6_applicability.text: Atlas entry MILL-PX-A4-ns-triple-resonance (line 106989) already records the d=3 match: Sym²(R^3)=6=n, Λ²(R^3)=3=n/φ, Onsager 1/3. The present artifact confirms this is the UNIQUE dimensional match; d=7 arises only via the arithmetic coincidence σ(6)−sopfr(6)=7 which belongs to BT-543 Yang-Mills β₀ rewriting, not to NS tensor structure. The atlas is internally consistent in flagging the MILL-PX-A4 entry as NEAR, not EXACT.

ladder_synthesis.structure: Atlas MILL-PX-A4 (NEAR, d=3 triple match) ⇢ [no known bridge] ⇢ d=7 claim (CONJECTURE, arithmetic only) ⇢ [no known bridge] ⇢ NS blow-up (OPEN, Clay)
unresolved: (UR-1) No PDE-level mechanism connects dim Sym²(R^3)=6 to NS blow-up scenarios (Beale-Kato-Majda, Escauriaza-Seregin-Sverak, Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg are all dimension-counting in different senses — none uses n=6 arithmetic).
(UR-2) The σ(6)−sopfr(6)=7 arithmetic is shared with BT-543 (Yang-Mills β₀ coincidence) and has no independent NS derivation.
(UR-3) Averaged NS (Tao 2016) permits blow-up but is not standard NS — substituting d=7 does not help.
(UR-4) Chen-Hou 2025 / Elgindi 2021 / Hou-Luo 2014 blow-up scenarios are 3D-specific (2D Boussinesq, 3D Euler); d=7 is not targeted by any current research program.
ladder_synthesis.bt_resolution: 0/1 MISS — NS blow-up open, d=7 prediction not empirically singled out

verdict.label: DERIVED
verdict.summary: BT-544 prediction '3중 공명 d=7' fails falsification on dimensional grounds. d=3 is the unique dimension in {3,...,9} where NS tensor spaces jointly satisfy n=6. d=7 appears only as σ(6)−sopfr(6) arithmetic coincidence and does not correspond to a distinguished dimension for NS triad interactions. Standard NS blow-up remains open (Clay); no progress on BT-544.
verdict.atlas_update_recommended: Downgrade any 'd=7 prediction' language around MILL-PX-A4 to explicit NEAR with counter-example CE-4 cross-reference. Keep MILL-PX-A4 as the d=3 near-match only.

round_2_options.direction: Revisit d=3 MILL-PX-A4: test whether Sym²=6 / Λ²=3 / alpha_c=1/3 jointly imply anything about BKM vorticity threshold (unlikely productive)
Tao-averaged NS with n=6-weighted nonlinearity: can we engineer a model where σ(6)·φ(6)=24 appears as a stability threshold?
Abandon BT-544 n=6 angle, record as confirmed MISS (consistent with atlas MILL-V4-T5-bt544-ns-supercriticality-barrier line 107183)

next_attempt_candidates.recommended: R2-C — BT-544 is confirmed non-접근 by this artifact; further n=6-driven attempts are low-yield. Focus shifts to BT-546 (BSD) where n=6 leverage is real.

honesty_statement.label: DERIVED
honesty_statement.text: This artifact is a prediction-verification attempt, NOT a proof or disproof of Navier-Stokes global regularity. No NS PDE was integrated. The dimensional scan uses only closed-form tensor-space dimensions and the canonical 2d-3 triad-manifold dimension. The conclusion is that the '3중 공명 d=7' phrasing fails a fair n=6 falsification test; d=3 is the unique n=6-resonant dimension, already recorded in atlas MILL-PX-A4. BT-544 resolution status is 0/1 (unsolved). This agrees with prior atlas assessment MILL-V4-T5-bt544-ns-supercriticality-barrier (line 107183: 'BT-544 Clay 문제는 n=6 구조와 수학적 연결 없음').
